name: World Book Day (UK)
description: A celebration of books and reading for children, celebrated by schools and libraries across the UK and Ireland.
notes: Different date from UNESCO’s World Book Day. Dress-up day in UK schools.

name: International Children's Book Day
description: Promotes children's books and inspires a love of reading.
organisation: International Board on Books for Young People (IBBY)
notes: Tied to Hans Christian Andersen's birthday.

name: World Autism Awareness Day
description: A United Nations observance that promotes understanding, acceptance, and support for autistic people.
notes: Observed every year on 2 April.

name: World Book and Copyright Day
description: A global celebration to promote reading, publishing, and copyright.
notes: Often just called World Book Day globally.

name: International Literacy Day
description: Raises awareness of global literacy issues.
notes: Highlights global literacy rates and challenges.

name: Libraries Week (UK)
description: Annual celebration of libraries, librarians, and their impact.
notes: Focus and themes change annually.

name: National Non-Fiction November (UK)
description: Celebrates factual writing for children.
notes: Unique UK event promoting real-world reading.

name: National Novel Writing Month (NaNoWriMo)
description: A global challenge to write a 50,000-word novel in a month.
notes: More focused on writing but ties into storytelling and literacy.

name: World Teachers' Day
description: Celebrates teachers and highlights their role in education and literacy.
notes: Often linked to literacy themes, though broader than books.

name: Christmas Day
description: Celebration of the birth of Jesus Christ, widely observed as a cultural holiday.
notes: Observed in many countries as both a religious and secular holiday.

name: Easter
description: Christian festival and cultural holiday commemorating the resurrection of Jesus from the dead.
date-rule: First Sunday after the first full moon on or after March 21
notes: Date varies each year based on the ecclesiastical approximation of the March equinox.

name: Valentine's Day
description: A celebration of love and affection between partners, friends, and family, often marked by gift-giving.
notes: Common gifts include flowers, chocolates, and cards.

name: Mother's Day (UK)
description: A day to celebrate and appreciate mothers and maternal figures, traditionally marked by giving gifts or flowers.
notes: Known as Mothering Sunday in the UK, different date from US Mother's Day.

name: Father's Day
description: A celebration honouring fathers and paternal figures, often involving cards and gifts.
notes: Date aligns with the US and many countries.

name: Halloween
description: A celebration involving costumes, trick-or-treating, and spooky themes. Common for sweets and small gifts.
notes: Gift giving is less formal but sweets and treats are exchanged.

name: Hanukkah
description: Jewish festival of lights celebrated over eight days, often involving daily gifts or treats.
date-rule: 25th day of Kislev (varies by Gregorian calendar)
notes: Eight nights of candle-lighting and often gift-giving.

name: Diwali
description: Hindu festival of lights, symbolising the victory of light over darkness. Often includes gifts and sweets.
notes: Widely celebrated in the UK with gifts, fireworks, and sweets.

name: Eid al-Fitr
description: Muslim festival marking the end of Ramadan, traditionally involves giving gifts and charity.
notes: Common to give Eidi (money or gifts) to children.

name: Chinese New Year
description: Marks the start of the lunar new year, celebrated with gifts, red envelopes, and family gatherings.
notes: Also known as Lunar New Year. Red envelopes with money are common gifts.

name: Boxing Day
description: The day after Christmas, traditionally associated with giving to those in need and modern-day shopping/gift exchanges.
notes: Now commonly a shopping day with sales and gift exchanges.

name: Earth day
description: An event to demonstrate support for environmental protection

name: Internation design day
description: An opportunity to recognise the value of design and its capacity to effect change.
notes: Used to be called World Design Day

name: International Women's Day
description: Celebrates women's achievements and advocates for gender equality.
notes: Often accompanied by events, speeches, and gifts or recognition.

name: Global Recycling Day
description: A global awareness day highlighting the importance of recycling and the role it plays in protecting the planet's future.
notes: Observed annually on March 18 to encourage better recycling habits and recognise recycling's environmental value.

name: World Mental Health Day
description: Raises awareness of mental health issues and promotes mental well-being.
notes: Focuses on specific themes each year.

name: International Coffee Day
description: Celebrates coffee as a beverage and promotes fair trade coffee.
notes: Often includes promotions at coffee shops.

name: National Cookie Day
description: Celebrates cookies and baking culture, often marked by giveaways and promotions from bakeries and food brands.
notes: Often treated informally as a Cookie Appreciation Day. Observed annually on a fixed date.

name: US Independence Day
description: Commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence in the United States, commonly celebrated with fireworks, parades, and community gatherings.
notes: Federal holiday in the United States, observed annually on July 4.

name: France National Day (Bastille Day)
description: France's national day commemorates the storming of the Bastille and is widely marked with military parades, fireworks, and public celebrations.
notes: France's national holiday on 14 July.

name: Canada Day
description: Canada's national day, observed with ceremonies, concerts, fireworks, and community events across the country.
notes: Celebrated on 1 July across Canada and by Canadians abroad.

name: India Independence Day
description: India's national day celebrating independence, marked nationwide with flag hoisting, speeches, and cultural events.
notes: Observed annually on 15 August.

name: Mexico Independence Day
description: Commemorates the start of Mexico's independence movement and is widely celebrated with civic ceremonies, the Grito, and public festivities.
notes: Marks the anniversary of the Grito de Dolores and the start of the independence struggle.

name: German Unity Day
description: Germany's national day marking reunification, celebrated with official ceremonies and public festivities across the country.
notes: Observed annually on 3 October.

name: Freedom Day (South Africa)
description: South Africa's national day commemorating the first democratic elections in 1994, observed with national reflection and celebration.
notes: Observed annually on 27 April.

name: Holi
description: Hindu festival of colors that celebrates spring, renewal, and the triumph of good over evil; often marked with colored powders, music, and shared sweets.
notes: Date varies yearly by the Hindu lunisolar calendar and is widely celebrated across South Asia and the diaspora.

name: World Afro Day
description: A global day celebrating Afro hair, culture, and identity while raising awareness of Afro hair discrimination.
notes: Established on September 15, 2017, and led by the World Afro Day campaign.

name: World Environment Day
description: A global day that encourages awareness and action to protect the environment.
notes: Led by UNEP and observed annually on June 5.

name: International Day of Friendship
description: A global observance that promotes friendship between peoples, countries, cultures, and individuals as a way to inspire peace and build bridges between communities.
notes: Proclaimed by the UN General Assembly and observed annually on July 30.

name: International Kissing Day
description: An informal global observance celebrating kissing as an expression of affection, connection, and intimacy.
notes: Also known as World Kissing Day. Observed annually on July 6.

name: World Cancer Day
description: A global awareness day focused on cancer prevention, detection, treatment, and equitable care.
notes: Global observance held every year on 4 February.

name: Mental Health Awareness Week (UK)
description: A UK-wide campaign that brings people together each May to focus on mental health and reduce stigma.
date-rule: May (annual week set by Mental Health Foundation)
notes: UK-focused awareness week. Different from the global World Mental Health Day on 10 October.

name: Dementia Action Week (UK)
description: A UK awareness campaign encouraging action on dementia, with a strong focus on diagnosis and support.
date-rule: May (annual week set by Alzheimer's Society)
notes: UK-focused campaign. Different from World Alzheimer's Day on 21 September.

name: Diabetes Week (UK)
description: A UK campaign that raises awareness of diabetes and highlights the priorities and experiences of people living with the condition.
date-rule: June (annual week set by Diabetes UK)
notes: UK-focused awareness week. Different from the global World Diabetes Day on 14 November.

name: World Heart Day
description: A global awareness day that promotes heart health and action to reduce cardiovascular disease.
notes: Global observance held every year on 29 September.

name: World Alzheimer's Day
description: A global awareness day that spotlights Alzheimer's disease and other dementias while challenging stigma.
notes: Global observance on 21 September. Distinct from the UK's Dementia Action Week.

name: World Diabetes Day
description: A global diabetes awareness day that brings together advocacy, education, and public health action.
notes: Global observance marked every year on 14 November.
